negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing full shared bounded eligibility rule: no env-controlled pool-vs-contract scan selection and no explicit max-obligations-per-block cap defaulting to 64.
related_but_insufficient_code: Port compute_obligations derives obligations from parent Scheduler state and uses due_index_bounds with SCHEDULER_MAX_TTL_BLOCKS=500.
No RITUAL_FORCE_CONTRACT_SCAN/synced scheduler pool fast path or RETH_MAX_OBLIGATIONS_PER_BLOCK default-64 cap was found.

rationale: This collapses the shared contract-state constraints, augmentation source-selection changes, synced-pool fast path, default contract scan, per-block cap, due-index optimization, and TTL bound into the effective scheduled-obligation discovery rule.
statement: Payload building and verification must derive due scheduled obligations from parent Scheduler and wallet state using the shared eligibility constraints, default to contract storage scans unless RITUAL_FORCE_CONTRACT_SCAN=0 and the scheduler pool is synced through the parent block, cap the obligation set with RETH_MAX_OBLIGATIONS_PER_BLOCK defaulting to 64, use due_index_bounds rather than full numCalls scans, and skip calls whose ttl exceeds the Scheduler protocol bound of 500 blocks.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create parent-state fixtures for each C1-C11 failure mode, more than 64 due obligations, synced and unsynced scheduler pool states, and ttl values 500 and 501; assert builder and verifier derive the same capped eligible set, use contract scan by default, use the pool path only for env=0 plus synced pool, and skip ttl 501.
Use a pathological call with numCalls=u32::MAX and a single due index; assert obligation computation checks only the due_index_bounds range and still respects executedBitmap and the per-block cap.
summary: Scheduled obligations share some parent-state eligibility, but capped/env-selected discovery is incomplete.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create >64 due obligations plus env-controlled synced pool cases; current port has parent-state due-window logic but no cap/env selection to satisfy the full invariant.
title: Scheduled obligations use shared bounded parent-state eligibility
